title: The Explanation
lyrics:
[verse]
Let me tell you about the thing
The thing that happened last Tuesday night
When the shadows grew long and the wind went quiet
And something moved in the fridge

[chorus]
Nobody knows what it was
Nobody knows where it went
Something strange and something old
Something... with mustard

[verse]
It crept across the kitchen floor
It left a trail of crumbs behind
The dog refused to look at it
The cat pretended to be blind

[chorus]
Nobody knows what it was
Nobody knows where it went
Something strange and something old
Something... with pickles now

[bridge]
Some say it was a sandwich from last week
Some say it was alive
Some say if you listen very carefully
You can hear it asking for a napkin

[chorus]
Nobody knows what it was
Nobody knows where it went
Something strange and something old
Something... goodnight
Don't check the fridge
style_prompt: storytelling folk song, mysterious, acoustic guitar, cello, soft percussion, 95 BPM, low dramatic vocal, spoken-word verses with sung chorus, campfire mystery, ominous but funny, like a spooky story that turns out to be about a sandwich
cover_description: A dark kitchen at night, fridge door slightly open with an ominous green glow coming from inside. A small pair of eyes peek out from behind a jar. On the counter, a trail of crumbs leads away from the fridge. A guitar leans against the fridge. Mysterious but clearly funny, not scary. Dark blues and greens with warm highlights.
